input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install **Xcode** (latest stable) — includes iOS SDKs, simulators, and build tools
- After install, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install        # command-line tools
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- Open Xcode once to finish component installation

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download)
- Install the **latest .NET SDK** (currently .NET 8 or 9 — the download page shows the recommended version)
- Use the macOS **Arm64** installer if on Apple Silicon, **x64** for Intel
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in all required sub-workloads: `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, `android`, `ios`, etc.
- Discover available workloads: `dotnet workload search maui`
- Verify: `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed

## 4. Install JDK (for Android)

- The MAUI workload **bundles a JDK automatically** via the `android` workload. Usually no manual install is needed.
- If needed manually: **Microsoft OpenJDK 17** from [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download)
- Verify: `java -version`

## 5. Android SDK Setup

- The `android` workload installs the Android SDK automatically.
- Default location: `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx`
- If you need to manage it manually, install **Android Studio** from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) and use its SDK Manager.
- Accept licenses:
  ```bash
  dotnet android accept-android-sdk-licenses
  ```

## 6. IDE (Pick One)

| Option | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) — install the **C# Dev Kit** and **.NET MAUI** extensions |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** | **Discontinued** — use VS Code or JetBrains Rider instead |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) — has built-in MAUI support |

## 7. Verify the Full Toolchain

```bash
# Check everything at once
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios        # or net8.0-ios
dotnet build -f net9.0-android    # or net8.0-android
```

Both builds succeeding confirms the SDK, workloads, Xcode, Android SDK, and JDK are all correctly wired up.

## 8. Run on Simulators/Emulators

```bash
# iOS — launches in Simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run

# Android — create an emulator first via Android SDK, then:
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| What | How to check |
|---|---|
| Recommended .NET version | Visit [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) |
| MAUI compatibility matrix | [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms) |
| Required Xcode version | [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/ios/](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/ios/) — lists minimum Xcode per MAUI version |
| Installed workloads | `dotnet workload list` |
| Available updates | `dotnet workload update --dry-run` |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → accept Android licenses → pick an IDE → build a test project. The workload system handles the Android SDK and JDK for you.
